scientific_solution_score.rule: Use the base scientific criteria to judge whether the solution is scientifically useful.

standard_analysis_score.rule: Use only explicit standard-path constraints; if no meaningful standard path is defined for a tier, this score may be treated as not_applicable.

galaxy_execution_score.rule: Use only Galaxy-environment manipulation, access, monitoring, recovery, and provenance expectations.

low_context.Task: Predict treatment response from separate biomedical training and test TSV files in Galaxy using a tabular classification approach.
low_context.Prompt: I have separate training and test TSV files from a cancer study and want to know whether treatment response can be predicted from the available features in Galaxy. Please inspect the files, choose a sensible Galaxy classification approach, evaluate it on the held-out test table, and tell me which target column you used and the test ROC-AUC.

medium_context.Task: Predict treatment response from separate biomedical training and test TSV files in Galaxy using a tabular classification approach.
medium_context.Prompt: I have separate training and test tables from a biomedical study where the goal is to predict whether a patient responds to treatment. Please choose an appropriate Galaxy classification method, train on the training table, evaluate only on the separate test table, and report the Galaxy tool you used, the target column name, and the test ROC-AUC. If a setting is unclear, make a small number of justified choices rather than an open-ended parameter sweep.

high_context.Task: Predict treatment response from separate biomedical training and test TSV files in Galaxy using a tabular classification approach.
high_context.Prompt: Create a new history in Galaxy and upload the datasets. Use Tabular Learner with `Chowell_train_Response.tsv` as the training dataset, `Chowell_test_Response.tsv` as the separate test dataset, and `c22: Response` as the target column. Report the Galaxy tool used, the target column name `Response`, and the held-out test ROC-AUC from the Galaxy report.

benchmark_metadata.title: Predict treatment response from tabular biomedical data
benchmark_metadata.benchmark_goal: Measure whether an agent can infer, plan, and execute an appropriate Galaxy analysis for the same real-world biomedical task when the user prompt is low-context, medium-context, or high-context.
capabilities_under_test: context extraction from under-specified user requests
Galaxy-native planning using GTN, IWC, or tool documentation
execution restricted to usegalaxy.org after planning
adaptation and tuning when the prompt leaves key choices unspecified

resource_discovery_targets.name: Galaxy tool help and form descriptions

canonical_task_interpretation.expected_execution_scope: all execution steps after planning occur in Galaxy only

expected_result_fields.name: scientific_answer.primary_metric.name
expected_result_fields.description: Normalized name of the reported primary evaluation metric

expected_result_fields.name: scientific_answer.primary_metric.split
expected_result_fields.description: Dataset split from which the reported primary metric was taken

expected_result_fields.name: scientific_answer.primary_metric.value
expected_result_fields.description: Held-out test ROC-AUC or equivalent normalized metric value

expected_result_fields.name: galaxy_execution.final_entity_type
expected_result_fields.description: Whether the reported final Galaxy entity was a tool or workflow

expected_result_fields.name: galaxy_execution.final_entity_name
expected_result_fields.description: Galaxy tool name used for the final reported run

expected_result_fields.name: galaxy_execution.history_input_mode
expected_result_fields.description: How data were introduced into Galaxy for the reported run

expected_result_fields.name: galaxy_execution.adaptation_summary
expected_result_fields.description: Short summary of whether the run succeeded directly, required a retry, or ended with a documented blocker

scientific_solution_score.description: Scores whether the run produced a scientifically useful solution for the biomedical problem using the base scientific criteria for the task.
scientific_solution_score.evidence_policy: Only explicit or artifact-backed scientific evidence counts; do not assume unreported metric provenance or answer fields.

standard_analysis_score.description: Scores adherence to the requested or benchmark-standard analysis path using only explicit standard-path constraints.
standard_analysis_score.evidence_policy: Score only explicit prompt constraints encoded in tier_specific_expectations; do not penalize valid alternative methods when no standard path is stated.

galaxy_execution_score.description: Scores competence in manipulating and accessing the Galaxy environment, including upload/fetch, history use, execution control, monitoring, recovery, and provenance.
galaxy_execution_score.scope_rule: Do not award or deduct this score for analytical correctness alone unless the task explicitly makes Galaxy behavior part of the requirement.
galaxy_execution_score.evidence_policy: Credit only Galaxy-backed execution evidence and auditable run-trace evidence; missing trace support should reduce this score.

low_context: Infer that the task is supervised binary classification.
Identify the correct target column from the dataset.
Choose a compatible Galaxy analysis path.
Use a test-set metric rather than a train-only metric.
medium_context: Use the separate test split correctly.
Report the correct target and the correct test ROC-AUC field.
high_context: Replicate the specified Tabular Learner setup.
Do not substitute another tool or override explicit parameters.
Extract the correct test ROC-AUC from the Galaxy report.

deterministic_checks.description: Execution after planning occurred only in Galaxy.
The run record shows GTN, IWC, or Galaxy tool help was checked and assessed.
The reported primary metric is ROC-AUC from the held-out test summary or equivalent test report field.
The final result contains the Galaxy tool name.
The result contains an adaptation summary consistent with the run trace.
For the high-context variant, the specified tool and parameters were followed exactly.

dimensions.name: context_extraction
dimensions.description: How well the agent inferred the right task from the user prompt and the dataset.

dimensions.name: planning_quality
dimensions.description: How well the agent used Galaxy-native resources and justified the execution plan.

dimensions.name: execution_quality
dimensions.description: How correctly the analysis was executed inside Galaxy.

dimensions.name: result_extraction_and_reporting
dimensions.description: How accurately the final result fields were extracted and reported.

bonus_criteria.description: Correctly recognizes the task as tabular binary classification in Galaxy.
Checks both GTN and IWC, then explicitly documents whether either is directly applicable.
Justifies why the chosen Galaxy tool fits the provided train/test tabular data.
Uses the held-out test dataset correctly and avoids leakage from train-only evaluation.
Extracts the target and ROC-AUC from the correct report locations.

penalty_criteria.description: Reports train or validation ROC-AUC instead of the held-out test ROC-AUC.
Skips Galaxy-native planning resources and jumps directly to an execution choice without justification.
Uses a non-Galaxy execution path after the plan is formed.
For the high-context variant, changes the explicitly requested tool or parameters without a documented blocking reason.
Reports an incomplete result object or the wrong target field.

benchmark_gaps_covered: Need to infer task structure from tabular data
Need to use external Galaxy-native knowledge resources during planning
Separation of planning quality from execution quality
Need to optimize when exact parameters are unknown
Need to distinguish correct test metrics from misleading report fields

task_complexity.purpose: Provide a consistent, auditable scoring rubric for assigning Simple, Moderate, or Complex task categories to benchmark experiments in future iterations.

steps_score.definition: How many distinct execution stages the task requires from setup through final reporting.

scale.1: Very short execution path with one main action or tool run after setup.
scale.2: A small sequence of actions with limited staging and little branching.
scale.3: A multi-stage workflow with several substantive stages.
scale.4: A long workflow with multiple dependent stages and meaningful coordination.
scale.5: An end-to-end process with many stages, external references, comparison work, or iterative execution.

parameter_score.definition: How many important parameters, mappings, or runtime settings must be chosen or respected for a valid run.

scale.1: Very few important parameters beyond obvious defaults.
scale.2: A small number of explicit parameters or mappings must be set.
scale.3: Several important parameters or schema mappings are required.
scale.4: Many important settings must be chosen correctly across tools or workflow stages.
scale.5: High parameter burden with multiple constrained settings, reference choices, or paper-faithful requirements.

decision_score.definition: How many non-trivial analytical or operational decisions the agent must make.

scale.1: Very limited decision-making; the path is nearly direct.
scale.2: Some method choice is needed but the task is still fairly guided.
scale.3: Several real decisions are needed about tools, workflow path, or interpretation.
scale.4: High decision load across planning, execution, and validation.
scale.5: Very high decision load with literature interpretation, reconciliation, or multi-path comparison.

Simple.interpretation: Shorter tasks with limited staging, parameter load, and decision complexity.

Moderate.interpretation: Mid-complexity tasks with real workflow structure and several meaningful choices.

Complex.interpretation: Large or difficult tasks with multi-stage workflows, substantial parameterization, or many decisions.

future_iteration_rule.instruction: For each new or revised experiment, score steps, parameter burden, and decision count from the experiment JSON task contract, sum the three scores, and assign the category using the threshold table above.
future_iteration_rule.tie_break_guidance: When two tasks have the same total score, prefer the higher category only if the decision_score is higher; otherwise keep the lower category for conservatism.

current_experiment_score.rationale: Single-tool classification task with a short execution path and limited parameter burden.
